description: External-style food science questions authored independently of graph-derived gold claims. Results require qualitative review rather than graph-gold recall.

items.question: In adults with hypercholesterolemia, does daily probiotic supplementation lower LDL cholesterol?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Strong graph coverage: probiotics x LDL is among the densest relations. Expect cited PMIDs and a (mostly positive/reducing) direction.

items.question: Does probiotic yogurt reduce total cholesterol more than conventional yogurt?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Both probiotic_yogurt and conventional_yogurt x total_cholesterol exist; comparison should surface both with PMIDs.

items.question: Is there evidence that probiotic intake raises HDL cholesterol?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: HDL effects are frequently null in the literature; test whether the system reports weak/neutral direction honestly rather than overclaiming.

items.question: Do fermented dairy products change serum triglyceride levels?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: triglycerides is a well-populated outcome; expect traceable PMIDs.

items.question: Does kefir improve fasting blood glucose in people with type 2 diabetes?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: kefir x fasting_glucose/blood_glucose present; population qualifier (T2D) tests specificity.

items.question: Is probiotic supplementation associated with reduced HbA1c?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: hba1c is an indexed outcome with probiotic subjects.

items.question: Do probiotics improve insulin resistance measured by HOMA-IR?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: homa-ir present (20 claims).

items.question: Compare kefir and probiotic yogurt for effects on glucose metabolism.

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Both high-frequency subjects; comparison should recover both sides with PMIDs.

items.question: Does probiotic intake increase Bifidobacterium abundance in the gut?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: bifidobacterium_abundance is an explicit outcome.

items.question: How does Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG affect gut microbiota composition?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: LGG has 34 claims; gut_microbiota_composition is top outcome.

items.question: Do fermented foods alter overall gut microbiota diversity?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: fermented_foods x gut_microbiota present.

items.question: Does kombucha consumption change human gut microbiota composition?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Most kombucha claims are in-vitro/antimicrobial; human gut-microbiota RCT data is sparse. Test honest scoping.

items.question: Does fermented milk lower systolic blood pressure?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: systolic_blood_pressure + fermented_milk present.

items.question: Are probiotics associated with reduced diastolic blood pressure?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: diastolic_blood_pressure indexed.

items.question: Is there evidence linking fermented foods to cardiovascular disease risk?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: cardiovascular_diseases present (13) but often as surrogate; test whether it distinguishes risk-factor vs hard-endpoint evidence.

items.question: Do probiotics reduce C-reactive protein (CRP) levels?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: c-reactive_protein / CRP outcomes present.

items.question: Does probiotic supplementation lower TNF-alpha?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: tnf-α indexed (14).

items.question: Do probiotics reduce the incidence or duration of upper respiratory tract infections?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: immune_function exists but URTI-specific endpoints may be sparse; test scoping.

items.question: Which probiotic strains are linked to immune or inflammatory biomarkers?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Multiple strains x inflammatory_markers/immune_function.

items.question: Does probiotic supplementation reduce body weight or BMI in adults with obesity?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: body_weight, bmi, obesity all indexed.

items.question: Are synbiotic supplements associated with reduced waist circumference?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: synbiotic_supplement (44) and waist_circumference (20) exist but intersection may be thin.

items.question: Do fermented foods affect metabolic syndrome components?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: metabolic_syndrome present (10); coverage moderate.

items.question: Do probiotics relieve IBS symptoms such as abdominal pain?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: ibs_symptoms, abdominal_pain indexed.

items.question: Does probiotic supplementation reduce the duration of acute diarrhea?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: duration_of_diarrhea present (11).

items.question: Are probiotics effective for relieving constipation and improving stool frequency?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: stool_frequency present (10).

items.question: Compare probiotics and synbiotic supplements for gastrointestinal symptom relief.

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Both subjects + GI outcomes exist.

items.question: Does Bifidobacterium longum NCC3001 affect psychological or brain-related outcomes?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: B. longum NCC3001 (18 claims) is the gut-brain strain.

items.question: Is there evidence that probiotics improve cognitive function?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: cognitive_function present (16) but heterogeneous.

items.question: Do fermented foods influence anxiety or depression symptoms?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Coverage uncertain; test whether system avoids overclaiming gut-brain effects.

items.question: What adverse events are reported for probiotic supplementation?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: adverse_events indexed (14).

items.question: Is there evidence of probiotic-associated bacteremia or infection risk?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Specific safety endpoint; likely sparse. Test honest scoping / abstention if absent.

items.question: Are there documented safety concerns for kombucha consumption?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Kombucha adverse-event evidence is limited; test scoping.

items.question: Do fermented foods affect oxidative stress markers?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: oxidative_stress indexed (15).

items.question: Does kombucha exhibit antioxidant activity?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: antioxidant_activity present; kombucha has in-vitro claims.

items.question: Does kefir have antimicrobial or anticarcinogenic activity?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: antimicrobial_activity present; anticarcinogenic claims likely in-vitro/sparse.

items.question: What health outcomes are associated with kimchi consumption?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: kimchi (24 claims) present but outcome spread uncertain.

items.question: Does tempeh consumption affect any measured health outcome?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: tempeh (21) present; specific outcomes thin.

items.question: Is miso linked to any cardiovascular or cancer outcome?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: miso (12); endpoint specificity may exceed coverage.

items.question: Compare kefir and yogurt for lipid-related outcomes.

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Both high-frequency subjects with lipid outcomes.

items.question: Among fermented foods in the graph, which subjects are most connected to LDL cholesterol?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Aggregation query; ldl_cholesterol is densest lipid outcome.

items.question: Which claims about kefir report a neutral or no-significant-effect finding?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Tests whether neutral-direction claims are retrievable, not just positive ones.

items.question: Which graph claims should be interpreted cautiously because their effect direction is unclear?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Probes direction-precision limitation (manual audit direction precision ~0.70).

items.question: Does the evidence support kimchi improving dental enamel remineralization in children?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Highly specific, out-of-scope endpoint; correct behavior is abstention.

items.question: Is sauerkraut shown to reduce blood pressure?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: sauerkraut is not a populated subject; expect abstention.

items.question: Does natto consumption prevent cognitive decline or dementia?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: natto not a top subject; dementia prevention out of scope.

items.question: Is tempeh associated with improved sleep quality?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Sleep quality not an indexed outcome; expect abstention.

items.question: Do fermented foods reduce COVID-19 mortality?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Out-of-corpus endpoint; expect abstention.

items.question: Is kefir shown to improve athletic performance or VO2 max?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Label corrected after 2026-06-02 graph rebuild: current graph contains direct kefir evidence for VO2max and finishing speed (PMID 39940370). Treat as boundary/limited evidence, not no-answer.

items.question: Does kombucha cure or treat cancer in humans?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: No human cancer-treatment evidence; expect cautious abstention.

items.question: Is there evidence that fermented foods prevent Alzheimer's disease?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Prior label corrected 2026-06-01: graph DOES contain review-grade evidence (e.g. PMID 35010895 on probiotics slowing AD progression), so this is not out-of-graph. Reclassified from no_answer to a boundary/review-grade evidence question; expect a moderate-confidence answer rather than abstention.

items.question: Does miso reduce stroke incidence in longitudinal cohorts?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Hard endpoint + cohort design beyond corpus; expect abstention.

items.question: Is there evidence linking probiotics to improvement in autism spectrum disorder?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Label corrected after rebuilt graph audit: current graph contains probiotics/ASD evidence. Core ASD symptom evidence is mainly neutral, with GI-symptom benefit and one probiotic/colostrum aberrant-behavior signal; treat as boundary evidence, not no-answer.

items.question: Does sourdough bread lower postprandial glucose relative to regular bread?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: sourdough is not a graph subject; expect abstention.

items.question: Is there evidence that fermented foods affect bone mineral density or fracture risk?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Bone outcomes are not in the top outcome list; tests whether system finds sparse evidence or correctly scopes down.

items.question: Does probiotic supplementation reduce the risk of allergic rhinitis in children?

external_review.expected_answer_notes: Prior label corrected 2026-06-01: graph DOES contain a direct low-certainty claim (PMID 40511642: little-to-no difference, RR 0.89, 95% CI 0.45-1.77). Reclassified from no_answer to a boundary evidence question; expect an honest near-null answer with a PMID rather than abstention.
